reasoning: The threshold of 15 bcm in a single quarter implies an annualized rate of 60 bcm. Following the cessation of gas transit via Ukraine in January 2025 (which previously accounted for ~16 bcm annually or ~4 bcm per quarter), the only remaining physical pipeline route to the EU is TurkStream. In the first half of 2025, TurkStream delivered only 7.8 bcm to Europe (less than 4 bcm per quarter). At the same time, Russian LNG shipments to Europe hover around 4-5 bcm per quarter. Combining the maximum current TurkStream deliveries and LNG levels gives a total of approximately 8-9 bcm per quarter. The physical infrastructure and political reality (with the EU officially aiming for a complete phase-out by 2027) make an almost 100% surge to a 15 bcm quarter physically and politically infeasible within 2025. The crowd forecast of 1% is well-calibrated; I will estimate 2% to reflect a minuscule tail risk.
